generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor embedded in building infrastructure to measure environmental temperature for real-time monitoring and control logic execution"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor measuring relative humidity levels within building spaces, integrated with BIM for environmental monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion/presence detection sensor for occupancy-aware building automation, enabling dynamic lighting and HVAC control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACControllerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller interface sensor monitoring HVAC system status and operational parameters for real-time synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACControllerDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Ambient light sensor for adaptive lighting control systems, integrated with building automation protocols"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingDataTransmitted
]

instance SecuritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Security system interface sensor providing real-time status of access control and perimeter protection systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecurityDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance HVACControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACControllerSensor
]

instance LightingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingSensor
]

instance SecurityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecuritySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static architectural representation derived from BIM serving as spatial foundation for DT operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMPlatformEnabler
]

instance BCSBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic control logic model defined by Building Control Engineers encapsulating sensor-actuator interactions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance DeviceConfigurationSchema : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured data schema for BCS components (sensors, actuators) extracted via MDE for semantic consistency"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized bidirectional data between physical building and DT including sensor readings and actuator commands"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, HVACControllerDataTransmitted, LightingDataTransmitted, SecurityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance DeviceMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured metadata for BCS components including operational parameters and semantic annotations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DeviceConfigurationSchema
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMPlatformEnabler
]

instance CustomServiceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics and plugin data integrated through modular architecture extension points"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModularArchitectureEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMPlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic foundation platform for device modeling and lifecycle management, augmented to support dynamic BCS components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, LifecycleManagementService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models generating deployable DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedArtifactGenerationService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data synchronization protocol ensuring low-latency bidirectional exchange between physical and digital systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, OperationalControlService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin protocol for continuous synchronization and state management of building control components"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization platform providing rich interactive interfaces for monitoring and control operations"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, OperationalControlService
]

instance ModularArchitectureEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized extension points enabling seamless integration of custom services and plugins for DT evolution"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceDevelopmentService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables semantic modeling of BCS components within BIM environment creating structured representations for DT transformation"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationInsight, ControlLogicDefinitionAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates continuous synchronization between physical building and DT ensuring up-to-date system states"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance OperationalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows direct interaction with physical infrastructure via DT enabling remote or automated control of building systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACAdjustmentAction, SecurityProtocolActivationAction
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports development and integration of custom services/plugins extending DT functionality through modular architecture"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight, DomainSpecificExtensionAction
]

instance AutomatedArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Utilizes MDE principles to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models automatically generating deployable DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentArtifactInsight, CodeGenerationAction
]

instance LifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Covers entire DT deployment and operation lifecycle from initial device modeling to runtime evolution"
    DTDFVocab:provides LifecyclePhaseInsight, EvolutionManagementAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Domain-specific service for predictive maintenance analytics integrated through modular architecture extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceAlertInsight, OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DeviceConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured representation of BCS components with semantic annotations for DT transformation"
]

instance ControlLogicDefinitionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Definition of control logic bridging static BIM models with dynamic DT requirements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Up-to-date system states including sensor readings and actuator statuses for monitoring operations"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of operational anomalies through real-time synchronization validation"
]

instance HVACAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote adjustment of HVAC setpoints through DT interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SecurityProtocolActivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated activation of security protocols via DT control interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics results from integrated custom services"
]

instance DomainSpecificExtensionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Integration of new services through modular architecture extension points"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeploymentArtifactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated deployable artifacts from MDE transformation pipeline"
]

instance CodeGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of executable code and service configurations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LifecyclePhaseInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current phase in DT lifecycle (design, deployment, monitoring, extension)"
]

instance EvolutionManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Incremental extension of DT through custom service integration"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance alerts generated from operational data analysis"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for system optimization based on analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system actuator executing commands for temperature and air quality control derived from DT control logic"
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting system actuator implementing adaptive control based on occupancy and ambient light sensors"
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

instance SecurityActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Security system actuator executing access control and emergency protocols via DT commands"
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

instance ValveActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC valve actuator with operational constraints encoded in DT behavioral models for safe operation"
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

instance DamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Airflow damper actuator with proportional control logic implemented through DT interface"
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

// C1: System Environment
instance BuildingControlSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Facility management and building automation operational domain"
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, HVACControllerSensor,
                   LightingSensor, SecuritySensor, HVACActuator, LightingActuator,
                   SecurityActuator, ValveActuator, DamperActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system involves **real-time control commands** generated by the digital twin and executed on physical building systems. Key components include:
  - **Command Types**: Operational adjustments (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting controls, security system activations) derived from DT analytics or user inputs (e.g., *Facility Managers*).
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats enabled by the framework’s modular architecture, though specific formats (e.g., JSON, XML, binary) are not specified.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied by the framework’s emphasis on **scalability** and **maintainability** (NF_X), suggesting robust access control and command validation to prevent unauthorized or erroneous actions.
  - **Feedback Systems**: Bidirectional data exchange ensures **closed-loop control**, where physical system responses (e.g., sensor confirmations, actuator statuses) are fed back to the DT for continuous synchronization.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Likely handled via the DT Framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without redeployment, though specific algorithms (e.g., priority-based, time-stamped) are not described.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Implicit through real-time synchronization, but no explicit acknowledgment protocols (e.g., handshake mechanisms, logging) are mentioned."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates on a **real-time synchronization** paradigm, enabling continuous data exchange between the physical asset (e.g., a building) and its virtual representation. This is critical for delivering **real-time insights** and **predictive analytics** across the infrastructure lifecycle, from design to operations. The architecture emphasizes **live synchronization** of static models (e.g., Building Information Modeling, BIM) with runtime data to ensure temporal alignment. While specific synchronization frequencies or latency thresholds are not explicitly quantified, the system’s design inherently prioritizes **low-latency, high-fidelity data flows** to support dynamic decision-making. The temporal granularity is dictated by the operational needs of the AECO domain, where **sub-second to near-real-time updates** are implied for critical services like predictive maintenance or adaptive energy management. The absence of explicit latency requirements suggests a focus on **adaptive synchronization** tailored to the heterogeneity of infrastructure environments, though the underlying middleware (e.g., Eclipse Hono/Ditto) is expected to enforce deterministic communication patterns for time-sensitive applications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain is characterized by **orchestration and coordination** of a **cyber-physical information system** that integrates **static models** (e.g., BIM—Building Information Modeling) with **runtime data** to create a **virtual representation** of physical assets. The architecture must support **component integration** across **heterogeneous infrastructure environments**, where models and data are **fragmented** and domain-specific. **System-wide coordination mechanisms** are critical to ensure **real-time insights**, **predictive analytics**, and **enhanced decision-making**, particularly for **data-driven infrastructure management**. The document implies the need for **resource management** to handle the **complexity of lifecycle phases** (design, construction, operation, refurbishment) while addressing **latency and synchronization challenges** between physical and digital domains. The **architecture pattern** is not explicitly defined, but it must accommodate **modularity**, **interoperability**, and **scalability** to bridge gaps between fragmented data sources and models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention, ensuring seamless transition from design to operational phases.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling **real-time data exchange** between the physical building and its digital twin, synchronized continuously.
  4. **Operation & Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and **operate physical infrastructure**, while **interactive visualization** provides immersive interfaces for oversight.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs.
  Governance is implicit through **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points**, ensuring adaptability and compliance with stakeholder-specific requirements (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system evaluates fidelity through the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which quantifies how accurately the DT reflects its physical counterpart. Validation and verification are implicitly supported by the framework’s **real-time data exchange** capabilities, ensuring continuous synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin. While the document does not specify detailed **uncertainty sources** (e.g., synchronization delays or model approximation errors), the system’s reliance on **automated transformation** of BIM models and **structured data extraction** via MDE principles minimizes manual errors. Quality assurance is embedded in the **modular architecture**, where standardized extension points enforce consistency in custom services and analytics. Facility Managers monitor system states and operational data, enabling **runtime validation** of the DT’s accuracy, though explicit error detection or correction mechanisms (e.g., recalibration routines) are not described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework operates as a **central hub** facilitating bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital systems, leveraging a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. While the document does not specify **communication protocols, network topology, or security protocols**, it implies integration with **heterogeneous systems** (e.g., BIM tools, IoT devices, analytics services). The framework’s design prioritizes **interoperability** and **scalability**, suggesting support for distributed architectures (e.g., cloud/edge hybrid). No details on **bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or protocol-specific implementations** (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) are provided. The system’s reliance on **runtime configuration management** indicates adaptability to varying network conditions, but no explicit redundancy mechanisms or security protocols (e.g., OAuth, TLS) are mentioned."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **model-based deployment** approach, where enriched BIM models are transformed into operational Digital Twins via a **coordinated life cycle process**. Deployment leverages the **DT Framework’s modular architecture**, with standardized extension points for custom services and analytics. The system supports **runtime configuration management**, enabling adjustments to DT capabilities (e.g., new sensor integrations, updated control logic) without requiring full redeployment. While the document does not specify hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), the **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems implies a **distributed deployment model**. Availability and reliability are assured through **low-overhead maintainability** and **bidirectional synchronization**, though specific SLAs or redundancy strategies (e.g., failover mechanisms) are not provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The **horizontal integration** of the Digital Twin system in the AECO domain relies on **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to connect with **external systems**, including **enterprise systems**, **other Digital Twins**, and **domain-specific tools**. The document highlights **dependencies on external systems** due to the **fragmented nature of models and data**, necessitating **integration patterns** that ensure seamless communication across **heterogeneous environments**. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Industry 4.0 standards, or BIM-related formats like IFC) are not mentioned, the **interoperability challenge** is central to enabling **real-time data-driven operations** and **collaborative decision-making** across stakeholders. The system must support **bidirectional data flows** to maintain synchronization between physical assets and their digital counterparts, with **enterprise-level integration** ensuring alignment with broader organizational workflows and lifecycle management processes."
]
